benchmark: OmniFuse vs synaptic-memory — extra download-only BEIR/MTEB datasets
note: synaptic ships these only as download scripts (not committed data); fetched from HuggingFace via synaptic tests/benchmark/download_datasets.py. Lexical track: omni field-weighted BM25 vs synaptic FTS-only (run_public_dataset), MRR@10.
correction_2026_07_10: The previously published omnifuse numbers here were measured BEFORE the idf_pow (term-specificity emphasis) change and were never re-run against it — they were stale. Re-measured below under the shipped default idf_pow=1.5, and also at idf_pow=1.0 for reference. Being explicit: the emphasis that wins the core suite REGRESSES the two heavily multi-relevant sets.

datasets.name: FiQA
datasets.note: measured pre-idf_pow, NOT re-run; synaptic FTS not measured (57,638-doc SQLite ingest too slow on this 16GB box)

datasets.name: MultiLongDoc-ko
datasets.note: measured pre-idf_pow, NOT re-run; 193MB of long docs, omni capped doc text at 6000 chars (zero-infra RAM bound)

idf_pow_tradeoff.finding: Term-specificity emphasis (idf_pow>1) is a genuine Pareto trade, not a free win. It wins the core suite 10/10 across p in [1.3, 2.0], but on heavily MULTI-RELEVANT passage-IR corpora it hurts: betting the score on one rare term is the wrong strategy when dozens of documents are relevant.
idf_pow_tradeoff.guidance: idf_pow is now a documented knob: build_inmemory(..., vector_kwargs={'idf_pow': 1.0}) for heavily multi-relevant corpora. At p=1.0 MIRACL-ko is 0.9489 vs synaptic 0.9495 — a dead heat — but Ko-StrategyQA then flips to a loss, so 1.5 remains the best single global default (13/15 datasets).
idf_pow_tradeoff.methodology_note: An earlier sweep of this parameter was INVALID: idf_pow is a keyword-only default bound at function-definition time, so monkeypatching the module constant silently changed nothing. The numbers above patch BM25/BM25F.__init__.__kwdefaults__ and the sweep demonstrably varies.

conclusion: On generic flat-text BEIR/MTEB passage IR (no titles, no citation graph) OmniFuse and synaptic sit at BM25-family parity: 2 wins each of the 4 head-to-heads. OmniFuse's decisive wins are on STRUCTURED corpora (finreg citation graph, titled document sets) where field-weighted BM25F and graph-companion fusion have something to exploit. On unstructured, heavily multi-relevant passage IR neither system dominates, and omnifuse's IDF emphasis actively costs it MIRACL-ko.
honest_limitations: synaptic's private corpora (krra/assort/x2bee) are gitignored — not in its repo, cannot be run.
FiQA/MultiLongDoc synaptic-FTS not measured (ingest time / RAM on a 16GB shared box); their omnifuse numbers predate idf_pow and were not re-run.
MultiLongDoc omni used a 6000-char doc cap — omnifuse's zero-infra in-memory BM25 cannot hold 193MB of long docs on 16GB RAM; a disk backend would be needed for full text.
